questions.q: Explain the concept of land degradation. How does it affect agriculture? (5 marks)
questions.answer: Land degradation refers to the deterioration of land quality due to human activities or natural processes. It can lead to soil erosion, nutrient depletion, and reduced fertility, ultimately affecting agriculture. This can result in decreased crop yields, reduced farm productivity, and ultimately, food insecurity. (2 marks) Land degradation can be caused by factors such as deforestation, overgrazing, intensive farming practices, and climate change. (2 marks) The effects of land degradation on agriculture are numerous, including reduced crop yields, decreased soil quality, and increased costs for farmers. (1 mark)
marking_scheme: Concept of land degradation (1 mark)
Impact on agriculture (2 marks)
Causes of land degradation (1 mark)
Effects on agriculture (1 mark)

questions.q: What are the different types of land use systems? Explain their characteristics and advantages. (5 marks)
questions.answer: There are several types of land use systems, including intensive, extensive, and mixed farming systems. (1 mark) Intensive farming systems involve high levels of inputs and technology to maximize crop yields, while extensive farming systems rely on minimal inputs and natural processes. (1 mark) Mixed farming systems combine elements of both intensive and extensive farming. (1 mark) The characteristics and advantages of these systems vary, but they all have the potential to contribute to sustainable agriculture. (2 marks)
marking_scheme: Types of land use systems (1 mark)
Characteristics of intensive, extensive, and mixed farming systems (2 marks)
Advantages of land use systems (2 marks)

questions.q: How does irrigation affect agricultural productivity? What are the different types of irrigation systems? (5 marks)
questions.answer: Irrigation can have a significant impact on agricultural productivity, as it allows farmers to grow crops in areas where water is scarce. (1 mark) The different types of irrigation systems include surface irrigation, sprinkler irrigation, and drip irrigation. (1 mark) Surface irrigation involves flooding the land with water, while sprinkler irrigation uses sprinklers to distribute water. (1 mark) Drip irrigation involves delivering water directly to the roots of plants. (1 mark) The advantages of irrigation include increased crop yields and reduced crop failures. (2 marks)
marking_scheme: Impact of irrigation on agricultural productivity (1 mark)
Types of irrigation systems (2 marks)
Advantages of irrigation (2 marks)

questions.q: A farmer has 10 hectares of land with a rainfall of 500 mm per year. Calculate the amount of water that can be saved by using a drip irrigation system compared to a surface irrigation system. Assume that the drip irrigation system has an efficiency of 90% and the surface irrigation system has an efficiency of 50%. (5 marks)
questions.answer: To calculate the amount of water that can be saved, we need to first calculate the total amount of water required for irrigation. (1 mark) Total water required = 10 hectares x 500 mm = 5000 m^3 (1 mark) Next, we need to calculate the amount of water that will be lost with the surface irrigation system. (1 mark) Water lost with surface irrigation = 5000 m^3 x (1 - 0.5) = 2500 m^3 (1 mark) Now, we can calculate the amount of water that will be saved with the drip irrigation system. (1 mark) Water saved with drip irrigation = 5000 m^3 x (0.9 - 0.5) = 1500 m^3 (1 mark)
marking_scheme: Calculation of total water required (1 mark)
Calculation of water lost with surface irrigation (1 mark)
Calculation of water saved with drip irrigation (1 mark)
Final answer (1 mark)

questions.q: A farmer has a field of 5 hectares with a rainfall of 300 mm per year. Calculate the amount of water required for irrigation using a sprinkler irrigation system. Assume that the sprinkler irrigation system has an efficiency of 80%. (5 marks)
questions.answer: To calculate the amount of water required for irrigation, we need to first calculate the total amount of water required. (1 mark) Total water required = 5 hectares x 300 mm = 1500 m^3 (1 mark) Next, we need to calculate the amount of water that will be lost with the sprinkler irrigation system. (1 mark) Water lost with sprinkler irrigation = 1500 m^3 x (1 - 0.8) = 300 m^3 (1 mark) Finally, we can calculate the amount of water required for irrigation using the sprinkler irrigation system. (1 mark) Water required for irrigation = 1500 m^3 + 300 m^3 = 1800 m^3 (1 mark)
marking_scheme: Calculation of total water required (1 mark)
Calculation of water lost with sprinkler irrigation (1 mark)
Calculation of water required for irrigation (1 mark)
Final answer (1 mark)

questions.q: A farmer has a field of 8 hectares with a rainfall of 400 mm per year. Calculate the amount of water required for irrigation using a drip irrigation system. Assume that the drip irrigation system has an efficiency of 95%. (5 marks)
questions.answer: To calculate the amount of water required for irrigation, we need to first calculate the total amount of water required. (1 mark) Total water required = 8 hectares x 400 mm = 3200 m^3 (1 mark) Next, we need to calculate the amount of water that will be lost with the drip irrigation system. (1 mark) Water lost with drip irrigation = 3200 m^3 x (1 - 0.95) = 160 m^3 (1 mark) Finally, we can calculate the amount of water required for irrigation using the drip irrigation system. (1 mark) Water required for irrigation = 3200 m^3 + 160 m^3 = 3360 m^3 (1 mark)
marking_scheme: Calculation of total water required (1 mark)
Calculation of water lost with drip irrigation (1 mark)
Calculation of water required for irrigation (1 mark)
Final answer (1 mark)
